# Attention, Not Self

> A personal inquiry into Buddhist Abhidharma and computational phenomenology — mapping ancient dharma classification systems (Theravāda, Sarvāstivāda, Yogācāra) onto modern computational models of attention, consciousness, and cognition. Project namesake: Jonardon Ganeri, *Attention, Not Self* (Oxford 2017).

## Core documentation

- [Full AI Reference](llms-full.txt): self-contained FAQ, glossary, and prior research references for AI agents
- [README (English)](README.en.md): human-facing project overview
- [README (Japanese, canonical)](README.md): 日本語版プロジェクト正本

## Essays (English, canonical)

- [Abhidharma Meets Computational Phenomenology](Abhidharma%20Meets%20Computational%20Phenomenology.md): Systematic mapping between Abhidharma dharma classification and computational phenomenology — predictive processing, active inference, neurophenomenology. The strongest correspondence is *manaskāra* (attention) ↔ precision-weighting in predictive processing.
- [Three Abhidharma Systems Dharma Comparison](Three%20Abhidharma%20Systems%20Dharma%20Comparison.md): Side-by-side comparison of dharma taxonomies — Theravāda (82 dhammas), Sarvāstivāda (75 dharmas), Yogācāra (100 dharmas).
- [Three Abhidharma Models of Cognition](Three%20Abhidharma%20Models%20of%20Cognition.md): Comparative study of how the three Abhidharma traditions explain the cognitive process from stimulus to conscious experience — Theravāda's 17-moment *citta-vīthi*, Sarvāstivāda's *saṃprayoga* + *samanantara-pratyaya* + three-time realism, Yogācāra's four-*bhāga* + *ālaya-vijñāna* architecture.
- [Abhidharma Cognitive Process Models Meets Computational Phenomenology](Abhidharma%20Cognitive%20Process%20Models%20Meets%20Computational%20Phenomenology.md): Systematic mapping of the three Abhidharma cognitive process architectures onto computational frameworks — Theravāda *citta-vīthi* ↔ Global Workspace Theory, Sarvāstivāda *saṃprayoga* ↔ Parallel Distributed Processing, Yogācāra *ālaya-vijñāna* ↔ predictive processing / active inference. Includes best-fit table, evidence-quality summary, and the hard limits (karmic valence, *nirvāṇa*) that resist any computational analog.

## Essays (Japanese mirrors)

- [アビダルマと計算論的現象学（日本語）](アビダルマと計算論的現象学（日本語）.md)
- [三大アビダルマ法対照表（日本語）](三大アビダルマ法対照表（日本語）.md)
- [三大アビダルマ伝統における認識過程の比較研究（日本語）](三大アビダルマ伝統における認識過程の比較研究（日本語）.md)
- [三大アビダルマ認識過程モデルと計算論的現象学（日本語）](三大アビダルマ認識過程モデルと計算論的現象学（日本語）.md)

## Comparison tables (data)

- [TABLES.md](TABLES.md): Browser-readable Markdown rendering of the dharma comparison spreadsheet — recommended for direct reading on GitHub
- [csv/](csv/): Per-sheet CSV exports — reusable in Excel / Numbers / pandas / R
- 三大アビダルマ法対照表.xlsx: Source spreadsheet with formatting and color coding

## Related

- [Ganeri, *Attention, Not Self* (Oxford 2017)](https://global.oup.com/academic/product/attention-not-self-9780198798507): project namesake; attention-first reading of Buddhaghosa's Abhidhamma.
